title: Cisco ISE PassiveID — WMI / AD Agent / Syslog Provider Health

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(d) covers access control. PassiveID is the user-context source for NGFW access enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Maintain user-context source for downstream access enforcement.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.20 (Networks security) — user-context fidelity is critical for network controls.
AC-4 (Information Flow Enforcement) — user-context driven flow enforcement requires PassiveID.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Stop the AD Agent service on a Domain Controller. Within 5 min ISE emits 91020 (AD Agent connection lost). UC-17.1.45 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restart the AD Agent. ISE reconnects. Alert clears.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *PassiveID* category — codes `91000` (PassiveID provider connected), `91001` (provider disconnected), `91002` (provider error), `91003` (mapping ingested), `91010` (WMI session ended unexpectedly), `91020` (AD Agent connection lost), `91030` (syslog provider parse error). Field: `provider_type` (WMI/AD-Agent/syslog/SPAN/Endpoint/REST), `provider_name`, `mapping_count`. ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/passiveid/providerstatus` polled every 5 min, written to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:passiveid_health`.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (91000, 91001, 91002, 91010, 91020, 91030))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:passiveid_health")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event count(eval(MessageCode=="91001")) as drops count(eval(MessageCode=="91002")) as errors latest(provider_status) as status by provider_type, provider_name
| where drops > 1 OR errors > 0 OR status=="DOWN"
| sort - errors - drops
description: Detects PassiveID provider failures (WMI, AD Agent, syslog, SPAN, Endpoint, REST). PassiveID is how ISE acquires user-to-IP mappings without 802.1X — critical for non-supplicant networks where the user identity comes from AD logon events.
value: PassiveID is the user-context backbone for many NGFW deployments — Cisco FTD/FMC, Cisco Secure Firewall, third-party firewalls all consume PassiveID-derived user-to-IP mappings via pxGrid or REST. When PassiveID providers fail, downstream firewall policies that gate by user identity revert to anonymous treatment.
implementation: Confirm *PassiveID* logging category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `provider_name` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: providers DOWN. (2) Per-provider mapping rate timechart. (3) Top-N providers with errors.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **AD Agent restart during patch** — Brief drop+reconnect, suppress isolated events. (b) **WMI permission glitch on a single DC** — DC patch or GPO change may break WMI for one DC; reconnect after re-grant. (c) **Mapping rate drops at night** — PassiveID volume tracks user logon volume; off-hours legit drops aren't failures.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE PassiveID configured with at least one provider.
• `passiveid_providers.csv` with `provider_name`, `provider_type`, `is_critical`, `domain`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *PassiveID* category at INFO.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — PassiveID Health":
• Per-provider status tile.
• Mapping rate timechart.
• Top-N error sources.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on provider DOWN.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **WMI fails** — DC's WMI namespace permission for ISE service account. PowerShell `wmic` test.
• **AD Agent missing** — installer not deployed on DC; deploy or update.
• **REST provider failures** — endpoint URL/cert/auth issue; validate from PSN CLI.
grandmaExplanation: When a person logs onto a Windows computer at the office, ISE learns who's using which IP address by listening to the company directory. We watch this listening line — if it goes silent, ISE no longer knows who's who, and other security tools that depend on that info also stop working.
